rules.title: Prisma query may reference field not in schema
rules.description: A Prisma query selects or filters on a field name that doesn't appear in any model definition in schema.prisma. This will cause a runtime error.
rules.impact: Prisma client will throw an unknown field error at runtime.
rules.suggestedFix: Check schema.prisma for the correct field names, run prisma generate after schema changes.

rules.title: Route handler registered before auth middleware
rules.description: A route handler (app.get/post/etc.) appears in the source code before the auth middleware (passport, jwt, session middleware) is applied via app.use(). Express processes middleware in registration order, so the route won't be protected.
rules.impact: Routes registered before auth middleware are unprotected and can be accessed without authentication.
rules.suggestedFix: Move app.use(authMiddleware) before all protected route registrations, or add auth middleware inline per-route.

rules.title: req.body accessed without validation middleware
rules.description: An Express route handler accesses req.body properties without any visible validation middleware (express-validator, zod, joi, celebrate, yup) applied to the route or earlier in the middleware chain.
rules.impact: Unvalidated request body can cause crashes, type errors, or injection attacks.
rules.suggestedFix: Add validation middleware (express-validator, zod schema, or joi) before the route handler.

rules.title: Prisma relation access without include
rules.description: Code accesses a nested property on a Prisma query result (e.g. user.posts, order.items) but the query doesn't use include or select with nested fields. Prisma doesn't lazy-load relations.
rules.impact: Accessing un-included relations returns undefined, causing silent data loss or crashes.
rules.suggestedFix: Add include: { relationName: true } to the Prisma query, or use select with nested fields.
